generations.code:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    max_a = max(a)
    max_b = max(b)
    idx_max_a = a.index(max_a)
    b_of_max_a = b[idx_max_a]
    result = []
    for i in range(n):
        if a[i] == max_a or b[i] == max_b:
            result.append('1')
        elif b_of_max_a < max_b:
            result.append('1')
        else:
            result.append('0')
    print(''.join(result))

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        dominators = [(j) for j in range(n) if a[j] > a[i] and b[j] > b[i]]
        if not dominators:
            res.append('1')
        else:
            # Check if every dominator can be beaten by someone outside dominators set
            dom_set = set(dominators)
            can = True
            for d in dominators:
                beaten = any((a[k] > a[d] or b[k] > b[d]) and k not in dom_set for k in range(n) if k != d)
                if not beaten:
                    can = False
                    break
            res.append('1' if can else '0')
    print(''.join(res))

bugs.description: The decision logic incorrectly marks all non‑max‑a and non‑max‑b players as possible winners whenever the player with maximum a has a b value lower than the overall maximum b. This ignores the relative ordering of strengths and fails for cases where some players are dominated in both maps.

The decision logic incorrectly marks all non‑max‑a and non‑max‑b players as possible winners whenever the player with maximum a has a b value lower than the overall maximum b. This ignores the relative ordering of strengths and fails for cases where some players are dominated in both maps.

fixes.code:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    max_a = max(a)
    max_b = max(b)
    idx_max_a = a.index(max_a)
    idx_max_b = b.index(max_b)
    b_of_max_a = b[idx_max_a]
    a_of_max_b = a[idx_max_b]
    result = []
    for i in range(n):
        if a[i] == max_a or b[i] == max_b:
            result.append('1')
        elif b[i] > b_of_max_a and a[i] > a_of_max_b:
            result.append('1')
        else:
            result.append('0')
    print(''.join(result))

pipelines.final_code:
t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    max_a = max(a)
    max_b = max(b)
    idx_max_a = a.index(max_a)
    idx_max_b = b.index(max_b)
    b_of_max_a = b[idx_max_a]
    a_of_max_b = a[idx_max_b]
    result = []
    for i in range(n):
        if a[i] == max_a or b[i] == max_b:
            result.append('1')
        elif b[i] > b_of_max_a and a[i] > a_of_max_b:
            result.append('1')
        else:
            result.append('0')
    print(''.join(result))

t = int(input())
for _ in range(t):
    n = int(input())
    a = list(map(int, input().split()))
    b = list(map(int, input().split()))
    res = []
    for i in range(n):
        dominators = [(j) for j in range(n) if a[j] > a[i] and b[j] > b[i]]
        if not dominators:
            res.append('1')
        else:
            # Check if every dominator can be beaten by someone outside dominators set
            dom_set = set(dominators)
            can = True
            for d in dominators:
                beaten = any((a[k] > a[d] or b[k] > b[d]) and k not in dom_set for k in range(n) if k != d)
                if not beaten:
                    can = False
                    break
            res.append('1' if can else '0')
    print(''.join(res))
